paragraphs.text: I very much do not like the fact that you typically do not properly own the games you buy digitally. Both steam and the epic games store have a lot of people complaining about having been locked out of their accounts resulting in them losing access to their games. This is a common complaint you see on the consumer feedback platform trustpilot (both having a lot of dissatisfied customers there). Furthermore games often require access to a server controlled by the publisher to function and this results in people often losing access to games they paid for without having any real recourse. Publishers often do not want to let people use private servers since they are afraid of that ending up competing with new titles they are going to release resulting in lost sales (source: video games europe). Being able to actually own your games is very important given the recent price increase of xbox gamepass and earlier price increases of playstation plus. You do get proper ownership on GOG but i often when i want to buy a game it's not available there meaning there is no good legal option available. A lot of people end up resorting to piracy but that may get your computer infected with malware due to the site they are using for it not being safe (i am only aware of one safe site with a big library but it's very hard to gain access to for most people, takes over 3 months to get account typically). Personally i will probably be fine regardless since i tend to play single player games and i have the ability to get access games with the DRM removed via a safe platform (safer than steam) but most people are not as fortunate as i am.
